hand_profile.script_style: Humanistic cursive with clear, rounded forms and moderate slant, typical of 18th-century notarial hands in Spain. The script is legible, with consistent letter height and spacing, and some flourished capitals.

letter_formations.u_v: u and v are visually distinct; 'u' is rounded and 'v' is pointed.
letter_formations.f_s: f and s are visually distinct; 'f' has a crossbar, 's' is long-s in medial positions and round-s at word end.
letter_formations.s: both long-s (ſ) in medial positions and round-s at word end are present.
letter_formations.a: single-compartment, open at the top, resembling modern 'a'.
letter_formations.e: open, rounded, with a clear loop, similar to modern 'e'.

hand_profile.marginalia_position: left margin, vertical orientation and top left corner
hand_profile.marginalia_style: different hand, more cursive and compressed
special_challenges: Marginalia is in a different, less legible hand
Occasional ink bleed and paper damage at edges
curator_rules: u and v are used interchangeably
f and s are used interchangeably
ç old spelling is always modern z
accents are inconsistent — transcribe exactly as written
horizontal cap over letter means n follows
long-s and round-s both present — transcribe as written
abbreviation marks: transcribe the expansion not the symbol
